payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Source elements present in the ledger.

original_descriptions: Provide children visibility into task inventory status
Enable children to view weekly earnings summaries
Weekly operational cycle boundary for earned amount total access by child actors
Weekly task availability reset cycle for claiming operations

payload.evaluated_scope: All input material in this project

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Task claim and completion tracking
payload.justification: Signal describes a strategic process by which children claim and track task completion status.

payload.description: Task assignment and valuation
payload.justification: Parental definition of compensated task assignments represents a core strategic process establishing work-reward pairings.

payload.description: Compensation distribution
payload.justification: Signal references the strategic process by which parents distribute accurate compensation based on performance reporting.

payload.description: Pocket money task with monetary value
payload.justification: Tasks are the central thing of strategic importance—defined by parents, tracked and claimed by children, forming the basis of the compensation system. | Monetary values associated with tasks are strategically important attributes that drive the compensation model.

payload.description: Weekly earnings summary
payload.justification: Earnings summaries represent a strategic information entity aggregating child compensation data on a weekly cycle.

payload.description: Child performance report
payload.justification: Performance reports are strategically important information entities enabling parental oversight and payment decisions.

payload.description: Longitudinal task history
payload.justification: Historical task records constitute a strategic data entity supporting accountability and trend analysis over time.

payload.description: Weekly pocket money cycle
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference weekly periodicity for task tracking and earnings—a fundamental temporal cycle governing the enterprise process.

payload.description: Child stakeholder in pocket money system
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference children as primary actors who claim tasks, view inventory, and access earnings—a core stakeholder at strategic level.

payload.description: Parent stakeholder in pocket money system
payload.justification: Signals identify parents as strategic actors who define tasks, assign values, and access performance reports for compensation purposes.

payload.description: Enable children to claim and track pocket money tasks on a weekly basis
payload.justification: Signal SG049 directly expresses a fundamental strategic purpose of the enterprise system—enabling child users to interact with task-based compensation.

payload.description: Enable parents to define compensated task assignments with monetary values
payload.justification: Signal SG050 articulates parental motivation as a strategic driver—the ability to configure the task-reward system.

payload.description: Enable children to view task inventory status and weekly earnings summaries
payload.justification: Signal SG051 captures a strategic purpose focused on transparency of available and completed work for child users. | Signal SG052 identifies a motivational goal centered on financial transparency and incentive for child users.

payload.description: Enable parents to access child performance reporting for accurate compensation distribution
payload.justification: Signal SG053 establishes a clear parental need tied to the strategic purpose of fair and accurate payment processing.

payload.description: Support accountability and trend analysis through longitudinal task history access
payload.justification: Signal SG054 indicates a strategic requirement for historical data access supporting governance and performance improvement.

payload.description: Child claims completed task for reward tracking
payload.justification: Signal SG290 describes the core business process of children claiming tasks to track monetary rewards.

payload.description: Parent specifies task with monetary value
payload.justification: Signal SG291 defines the parent capability to create and assign monetary values to task entities.

payload.description: Display available and completed tasks to child
payload.justification: Signal SG293 specifies the visibility function providing children access to task status information.

payload.description: Calculate and display weekly earnings total to child
payload.justification: Signal SG294 defines the aggregation function computing and presenting weekly monetary totals to children.

payload.description: Generate parent report showing child-task-earnings relationships
payload.justification: Signal SG295 establishes the reporting function enabling parents to view comprehensive payment tracking data.

payload.description: Task availability resets at weekly boundaries
payload.justification: Signal SG296 specifies the cyclical business rule governing when task availability is refreshed.

payload.description: Historical task data must be retained and accessible
payload.justification: Signal SG297 mandates the business rule for data retention and accessibility across time periods.

payload.description: Task entity with monetary value attribute
payload.justification: Signal SG291 explicitly defines task entities with associated monetary value attributes specified by parents.

payload.description: Child entity
payload.justification: Signal SG292 identifies child as a distinct entity type participating in relationships with completed tasks.

payload.description: Completed task linked to child entity
payload.justification: Signal SG292 explicitly specifies the linkage relationship between completed task entities and child entities.

payload.description: Weekly aggregated earnings total for child
payload.justification: Signal SG294 defines a derived attribute representing the weekly sum of earned monetary amounts.

payload.description: Child-task-earnings relationship for payment tracking
payload.justification: Signal SG295 describes a composite relationship linking child, task, and earnings data for parent visibility.

payload.description: Historical task data entity
payload.justification: Signal SG297 establishes historical task data as persistent entities retained across temporal periods.

payload.description: Weekly task cycle
payload.justification: Signals SG294 and SG296 establish a weekly temporal cycle governing earnings aggregation and task availability reset.

payload.description: Weekly boundary triggers task availability reset
payload.justification: Signal SG296 defines the temporal boundary event that initiates the task reset process.

payload.description: Task completion event
payload.justification: Signals SG290 and SG292 reference task completion as a significant business event affecting claims and entity linkage.

payload.description: Child actor
payload.justification: Signals SG290, SG293, and SG294 collectively identify children as primary actors who claim tasks, view task lists, and see earnings.

payload.description: Parent actor
payload.justification: Signals SG291 and SG295 identify parents as actors who specify tasks and access reporting to fulfill payments.

payload.description: Track monetary rewards for completed tasks
payload.justification: Signal SG290 establishes the core business objective of tracking monetary rewards earned by children.

payload.description: Support parent payment fulfillment
payload.justification: Signal SG295 identifies payment fulfillment as a key business objective enabled by reporting capability.

payload.description: Child visibility limited to tasks within temporal scope
payload.justification: Signal SG293 defines a business constraint restricting which task entities are visible to child actors based on time.

payload.description: Historical data must remain accessible across periods
payload.justification: Signal SG297 mandates a constraint requiring persistent accessibility of historical information.

payload.description: Task completion event association rule linking completed tasks to child entity records
payload.justification: SG457 explicitly describes a logical association rule defining how task completion events are linked to child entities, directly mapping to a business rule at the Designer level.

payload.description: Data visibility rule governing child user access patterns to task collection data
payload.justification: SG458 specifies data visibility requirements as logical access patterns, representing a rule governing how child users interact with task collections.

payload.description: Weekly earned amount aggregation and presentation process for child users
payload.justification: SG459 describes temporal aggregation logic as a logical process that computes and presents weekly earned amounts to children.

payload.description: Parent aggregated reporting function for child earnings and task completion data
payload.justification: SG460 describes a logical reporting interface as a function that provides parents with aggregated views of child data.

payload.description: Weekly task availability state reset process
payload.justification: SG461 describes state reset logic as a logical process that restores task availability on a weekly basis.

payload.description: Child user to task collection visibility relationship
payload.justification: SG458 implies a logical relationship between child users and task collections through the access pattern specification.

payload.description: Weekly earned amount aggregate attribute for child entity
payload.justification: SG459 implies a logical data attribute representing the aggregated weekly earnings associated with child entities.

payload.description: Parent user to child earnings and task data aggregation relationship
payload.justification: SG460 implies a logical relationship between parent users and aggregated child data through the reporting interface.

payload.description: Historical task record entity
payload.justification: SG462 implies the existence of a logical entity representing historical task records that must be retained and accessed.

payload.description: Weekly task availability restoration cycle
payload.justification: SG461 explicitly describes periodic state reset logic defining weekly cycle behavior, directly mapping to a temporal cycle at the Designer level.

payload.description: Historical task record persistence and accessibility constraint
payload.justification: SG462 specifies a data retention requirement as a design constraint mandating that historical task records must remain logically persistent and accessible.

payload.description: Operating system version compatibility constraint for deployment
payload.justification: Signal explicitly describes a platform compatibility constraint specifying concrete OS versions for deployment, which is a technical constraint on the system at the Builder level.

payload.description: Weekly operational cycle boundary for task availability reset and earned amount total access
payload.justification: Signal SG542 establishes a weekly operational schedule boundary defining when child actors can access earned totals, representing a detailed timing specification. | Signal SG544 specifies the weekly operational cycle timing for task reset operations, representing a detailed sequencing specification.

payload.description: iOS mobile platform deployment node
payload.justification: Signal SG545 explicitly names iOS as one of three distinct deployment platform locations. Each platform is a separate named deployment node requiring individual specification. | Signal SG545 explicitly names Android as one of three distinct deployment platform locations. Each platform is a separate named deployment node requiring individual specification.

payload.description: Windows desktop platform deployment node
payload.justification: Signal SG545 explicitly names Windows as one of three distinct deployment platform locations. Each platform is a separate named deployment node requiring individual specification.

payload.description: Child user actor executing task claiming operations
payload.justification: Signal SG539 directly identifies children as operational actors performing task claiming activities. This is a distinct actor role in the system's detailed user interface specification.

payload.description: Parent user actor configuring tasks and assigning monetary values
payload.justification: Signal SG540 identifies parents as operational actors with responsibility for task configuration and value assignment activities. Distinct from child actor role.

payload.description: Child role with visibility permissions for available and completed task views within weekly cycles
payload.justification: Signal SG541 defines operational visibility requirements specific to child actors, representing a detailed role specification with defined access permissions.

payload.description: Parent role with report access permissions for pocket money distribution operations
payload.justification: Signal SG543 defines parent operational role in terms of report access and distribution activities, representing a detailed role specification distinct from the actor definition.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Expresses the fundamental purpose of enabling children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks weekly.
Articulates parental motivation to define compensated task assignments with associated monetary values.
Captures the purpose of providing children visibility into available and completed task inventory.
Identifies the motivational goal of enabling children to view their weekly earnings summary.
Establishes parental need to access child performance reporting for accurate compensation distribution purposes.
Indicates stakeholder requirement for longitudinal task history access supporting accountability or trend analysis.
Section header indicating the commencement of conceptual system description.
Establishes the core business concept of children claiming completed tasks for monetary reward tracking.
Defines the parent capability to specify task entities with associated monetary value attributes.
Specifies the relationship between completed task entities and child entities through linkage.
Defines child actor visibility requirements for both available and completed task entities within temporal scope.
Establishes weekly aggregation capability for earned monetary totals visible to child actors.
Defines parent reporting capability displaying child-task-earnings relationships to support payment fulfillment.
Specifies the cyclical business rule for task availability reset at weekly temporal boundaries.
Mandates retention and accessibility of historical task data entities across temporal periods.
Logical association rule defining how task completion events are linked to child entities.
Data visibility requirements specifying logical access patterns for child users to task collections.
Temporal aggregation logic defining weekly computation and presentation of earned amounts to children.
Logical reporting interface providing parents aggregated view of child earnings and task completion data.
Periodic state reset logic defining weekly cycle behavior for task availability restoration.
Data retention requirement mandating logical persistence and accessibility of historical task records.
Platform compatibility constraint specifying concrete operating system versions for deployment.
Identifies children as operational actors who execute task claiming activities throughout the week on a temporal basis.
Identifies parents as operational actors responsible for task configuration and monetary value assignment activities.
Defines operational visibility requirements for child actors viewing available and completed tasks within weekly cycles.
Establishes weekly operational schedule boundary when child actors can access earned amount totals.
Defines parent operational role in accessing reports to execute pocket money distribution activities.
Specifies weekly operational cycle timing when task availability resets for claiming operations.
Defines deployment platform locations specifying where the system operates across iOS, Android, and Windows environments.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Signal elements present in the ledger.
